metaTitle: Electrical Safety for Semiconductor Industry in Singapore
metaDescription: Optimize electrical protection in Singapore’s semiconductor industry with Carelabs’ relay coordination study.
h2: Making Power Systems Safe To Manage
h3: Ensuring Electrical Safety with Relay coordination in Singapore’s Semiconductor & Electronics Industry
What is Relay Coordination and How Does it Work ?
How it Helps the Semiconductor and Electronics Industry
paragraphs: Relay coordination is an essential part of an electrical protection system for reliable and selective fault isolation. In a healthy power system, the upstream and downstream relays communicate in coordination with each other to isolate the faults at the nearest possible point.

The relevance of relay coordination can be understood thus: if a fault occurs at any point in the power system, the nearest relay must trip first and isolate the fault thereby maintaining power supply continuity in unaffected areas.

During a relay coordination study, especially with Carelabs, a comprehensive electrical audit of the system is conducted using advanced technology like ETAP. Relevant data regarding equipment ratings, existing relay settings, system parameters, operating conditions, etc are collected and evaluated. Fault currents and load flow conditions are analyzed for different network configurations.

One of the key determining factors in relay coordination is time current curve characteristics. During a relay coordination study, TCC curves for different relays are plotted to get data for establishing proper coordination between the relays.

The findings and recommendations of the relay coordination study are documented based on compliance with local Singapore codes of electrical safety and international standards like OSHA, NFPA 70E, and IEC.

Carelabs, based out of Dubai, undertakes power system consulting services for businesses worldwide. We manage, consult, and advise the best practices to perfect your power systems and maintain uninterrupted operations.
